summary: Uneven first set salvaged by a soaring Comes A Time and solid second‑set
blurb: The show opens shakily with vocal and drum issues on In The Midnight Hour, but the band tightens in the second set delivering a standout Comes A Time jam and a crisp Drums>Space segment. West L.A. Fadeaway, Let It Grow, Candyman and Good Lovin' provide additional highlights.
review: June 20, 1986 at Berkeley’s Greek Theatre was a mixed‑bag affair that ultimately rewards listeners who can get past an uneven opening. The first set stumbled – Jerry’s solo on In The Midnight Hour sounded off, Bob’s vocals were loogy and the drums were overrun with nonstop fills – but flashes of brilliance peeked through in West L.A. Fadeaway and a nicely rendered Let It Grow. By the second set the Dead found their groove: Brent’s keyboard flourishes lifted Comes A Time into “blinding brilliance,” the Drums>Space segue showcased Mickey and Bill in tight, exploratory mode, and the band rode the momentum into a jubilant Good Lovin' rap with Jerry and Bob trading verses. The encore, The Mighty Quinn, closed on a high note, making this Greek show worth a spin for its strong second‑set moments despite the rough start.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings consistently praise the Comes A Time jam, Drums>Space, and strong second‑set flow, while also noting repeated weaknesses in the opening Midnight Hour and early vocal/drum issues. The net sentiment is modestly positive, yielding a moderate boost.

best_recording.reason: Decent soundboard capture with clear audio, full set present, and the only recording that balances overall listening quality despite some drum quirks.

key_highlights: Standout Comes A Time jam that lifts the second set.
Tight Drums>Space segment highlighting both drummers.
West L.A. Fadeaway and Let It Grow provide strong first‑set moments.
Good Lovin' rap adds humor and energy to the closing.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers solid leads early and a soaring solo on Comes A Time; initial struggles on Midnight Hour give way to confident playing later in the show.
band_performance.Phil: Provides steady bass foundation, shines on West L.A. Fadeaway and is warmly acknowledged during the band intro.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal performance starts rough (loogy on Midnight Hour) but improves markedly on Playing In The Band and Good Lovin' rap; adds humor in the good‑lovin' shout‑out.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work shines especially in Comes A Time, adding melodic flair; occasional generic comments about needing love but overall solid throughout.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums contribute to the highlighted Drums>Space segment and tighten up after a shaky first set; early nonstop fills noted as problematic.
band_performance.Billy: Shares drum duties with Mickey, suffers from over‑filled patterns in the opening set but settles into a tighter groove for the second‑set jams.
